strategic_profile.executive_summary: Wake Forest University is an elite private research institution known for blending liberal arts traditions with highly selective pre-professional programs. A pioneer in the test-optional movement, the university evaluates applicants holistically with a strong emphasis on academic rigor, character, and its 'Pro Humanitate' motto.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Wake Forest operates under a 'Work Forest' culture with rigorous grading policies, particularly in pre-health and pre-business weed-out courses.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students must be prepared for intensive academic pressure and potential grade deflation in lower-level prerequisite classes.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The university fills over half of its incoming class through Early Decision, creating a structural advantage for ED applicants.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Regular Decision is highly competitive, and waitlist movement relies heavily on demonstrated interest and full-pay capability.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Greek life is a dominant force on campus, with nearly half of the student body participating.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students seeking a vibrant, socially active environment will thrive, while those opting out may need to intentionally seek alternative social avenues.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Highly active, Greek-dominated social scene with deep traditions like 'rolling the quad' after athletic victories. The environment is described as work-hard, play-hard.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Located on a 340-acre campus in suburban Winston-Salem. The location provides a classic, enclosed campus feel with easy access to downtown Winston-Salem and the Innovation Quarter.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Designated as an R2 institution with high research activity, heavily intertwined with the Atrium Health Wake Forest Baptist medical system and Wake Downtown initiatives.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional since 2008. Standardized scores are not required but will be reviewed holistically if submitted. Enrolled students who submitted scores must provide official verification.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Record-high application volume. Waitlist saw significant movement, constituting about 6% of the incoming class.

gpa.notes: Wake Forest does not publicly report exact GPA distributions, but 96% of the admitted Class of 2028 were in the top 20% of their graduating class.

testing.policy_note: 54% of admitted students for the Class of 2029 did not submit test scores.

colleges.name: Wake Forest College
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students pursuing liberal arts, pre-med, or humanities who want small class sizes and close faculty mentorship.
colleges.housing_profile: Three-year on-campus housing requirement ensures a tight-knit residential community.

majors.name: Political Science and Government
majors.notes: One of the most popular majors with strong pre-law placement.

majors.name: Communication
majors.notes: Requires an overall minimum GPA of 2.0 in all attempted communication courses.

majors.name: Biology
majors.notes: Pre-health track features significant grade deflation and intense weed-out courses.

colleges.name: School of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: For ambitious future corporate leaders aiming for elite placements in Big 4 accounting, investment banking, or management consulting.

majors.name: Finance
majors.special_requirements: Must complete 49 total credit hours by the conclusion of sophomore spring term to apply.
majors.notes: Extremely strong Wall Street placement pipeline. Students must maintain a high GPA across weed-out prerequisites to gain admission mid-sophomore year.

supplemental_requirements.details: Requires four short responses: 1) Intellectual curiosity (150 words), 2) Dr. Maya Angelou quote reflection (300 words), 3) Top Ten list (150 words), 4) List of five intriguing books read.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Wake Forest heavily evaluates applicants based on their alignment with 'Pro Humanitate' and places tremendous weight on the optional video interview to assess character and fit.

major_selection_tactics: Apply with a liberal arts focus initially, as all students enter undeclared and apply to the business school during sophomore year.
Highlight interdisciplinary interests in essays to align with Wake's liberal arts core.
Emphasize service and community engagement to directly appeal to the Pro Humanitate ethos.
college_ranking_tactics: Apply Early Decision to maximize chances, as Wake Forest historically fills a vast majority of its class through ED rounds.
Conduct the optional video interview to build a personal connection and robust demonstrated interest.
If waitlisted, submit a strong Letter of Continued Interest (LOCI) within 10 days and explicitly state no need for financial aid if applicable, as waitlist admissions are need-aware.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: N/A - Students do not declare majors until their sophomore year.

scholarships.name: Signature Scholarships (Carswell, Gordon, Graylyn, Reynolds, Stamps)
scholarships.benefits: Covers tuition, room, and board. Includes enrichment stipends, a 4-year on-campus residency requirement, and submission of a reflective 'Apologia'.

scholarships.name: Leadership & Character Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Provides funding and programming for students dedicated to civic engagement and character leadership.

ap_policy.general_rule: Scores of 4 or 5 are generally required for credit.
exceptions: AP Economics requires taking both Micro and Macro tests and earning a combined score of 8 or higher.
ap_policy.usage: AP credits count toward the 120 hours needed for graduation but DO NOT satisfy the university's divisional or core requirements.

ib_policy.general_rule: Credits accepted for high-level exams with sufficient scores, similarly counting only toward graduation hours.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Wake Forest does not maintain articulation agreements with any college. A maximum of 60 transfer hours is permitted, and at least 60 hours must be completed at Wake Forest.

what_it_takes: Extremely strong academic rigor with a high number of AP/IB classes.
Demonstrable commitment to service and the broader community.
Willingness to engage deeply with the admissions office via interviews and LOCIs.
Ability to thrive in a work-hard, play-hard, high-pressure environment.
common_activities: Community Service (Hit the Bricks, Wake 'N Shake)
essay_tips: Use the Top Ten list to showcase raw personality and unique quirks, avoiding generic topics.
Connect the Maya Angelou quote tangibly to a lived experience rather than providing a literal or philosophical analysis.
Frame intellectual curiosity around genuine, specific rabbit holes rather than just naming a school subject.
red_flags: Showing zero demonstrated interest or skipping the optional interview.
Low grades in quantitative subjects if aiming for the School of Business.
Lack of community service or character-driven extracurriculars.
insights: Wake Forest is deeply relational; applying Early Decision provides a massive advantage.
Grade deflation is a real concern for pre-med and pre-business students, requiring intense focus during freshman and sophomore prerequisite courses.
The 'Work Forest' moniker is earned; students must balance heavy academic workloads with a highly active Greek-dominated social life.
